concept: Distant / Far Away / Remote in Space
description: Far away in space or time; not close. 'Thaeru' names the quality of remoteness—separation by space, the far that requires journey to reach.
sound_symbolism: The breathy fricative /θ/ opens the word, and the hiatus 'a.e' stretches its middle across two separate vowels—a small distance to cross before 'ru'. The close back 'u' ends the word low and far.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'thaeru lokue' (distant place). Distinct from preposition 'woe' (far) which marks spatial relationship. This is the quality of being far.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: What seems distant may be near—enlightenment is not far, only obscured by confusion.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Distant places required journey—remoteness meant time, effort, and risk to reach.
